text:
There once was a little girl named Holly who discovered she had the extraordinary talent to transform ordinary things into something magical. Her favorite pastime was weaving spells that made flowers bloom year-round, hummingbirds whistle opera, and cats knit perfect sweaters.

One sunny afternoon, while playing near an old, gnarled apple tree, Holly felt a tap on her shoulder. Looking around, she saw nothing. Curious, she followed the sound.

The tree whispered, "Oh dear little girl, use your magic wisely."

"What?" Holly asked.

The tree continued, "You've been changing things. Flowers have tiny voices, birds have tunes. We all have our quirks."

Holly thought that sounded strange. She bent closer to listen to a particularly mellifluous warbling bird. It sang about friendship and adventures. Deciding the birds needed a good listener, Holly decided to sing something special just for them.

A few days later, the entire forest was full of cheerful singing birds.

H
